revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=全州神社| 所在地=朝鮮全羅北道全州府華山面| 祭神=天照大神
明治天皇
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）}} '''全州神社'''（ぜんしゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代 (朝鮮)|日本統治時代]]の[[朝鮮]][[全羅北道 (植民地時代)|全羅北道]][[全州府]]（現 [[大韓民国]][[全羅北道]][[全州市]]）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[明治天皇]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）[[9月29日]]に創建され、[[1943年]]（[[昭和]]18年）[[5月1日]]に国幣小社に列格した。[[1945年]]の[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[日本]]の敗戦の後、廃座された。 [[Category:朝鮮の神社|せんしゆうしんしや]] [[Category:国幣小社|せんしゆうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=全州神社| 所在地=朝鮮全羅北道全州府華山面| 祭神=天照大神

明治天皇
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）}} '''全州神社'''（ぜんしゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[全羅北道 (日本統治時代)|全羅北道]][[全州市|全州府]]（現・[[大韓民国]][[全羅北道]]全州市）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[明治天皇]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）[[9月29日]]に創建され、[[1943年]]（[[昭和]]18年）[[5月1日]]に国幣小社に列格した。[[1945年]]の[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[敗戦]]の後、廃座された。 {{DEFAULTSORT:せんしゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない建築物]] {{shinto-stub}} [[zh:全州神社]]

revision.comment: 訂正と補足
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=全州神社| 画像 =[[Image:Zenshu Shrine.JPG|250px|center]]| 所在地=朝鮮全羅北道全州府華山面| 祭神=天照大神
明治天皇
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）| 例祭=10月14日}} '''全州神社'''（ぜんしゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[全羅北道 (日本統治時代)|全羅北道]][[全州市|全州府]]（現・[[大韓民国]][[全羅北道]]全州市）にあった[[神社]]である。[[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[明治天皇]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]。旧[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）、府内の多佳山山頂に鳥居を建設し皇大神宮の遙拝所とした「神社明細帳（全州神社）」 JACAR（アジア歴史資料センター） Ref.A03010213400 （国立公文書館）。 [[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）、在住日本人の間で全州神社を創立することで衆議が決定したため、道内で寄付を集め、同年[[11月24日]]に本殿以下の建物が竣工した。[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[2月21日]]、諸設備の完成を待って神社創立を出願し、神社寺院規則（大正4年朝鮮総督府令第82号）に基づいて、同年[[9月27日]]、天照大神を祭神とする全州神社の創立が許可された神社創立許可 朝鮮総督府官報第1248号（大正5年9月29日） 424頁。 [[1943年]]（[[昭和]]18年）[[5月1日]]、国幣小社に列格された。[[1945年]]の[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[敗戦]]の後、廃座された。 ==脚注== {{DEFAULTSORT:せんしゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない建築物]] {{shinto-stub}} [[zh:全州神社]]

revision.comment: /* 歴史 */ 訂正と補足
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=全州神社| 画像 =[[Image:Zenshu Shrine.JPG|250px|center]]| 所在地=朝鮮全羅北道全州府華山面| 祭神=天照大神
明治天皇
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）| 例祭=10月14日}} '''全州神社'''（ぜんしゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[全羅北道 (日本統治時代)|全羅北道]][[全州市|全州府]]（現・[[大韓民国]][[全羅北道]]全州市）にあった[[神社]]である。[[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[明治天皇]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]。旧[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）、府内の多佳山山頂に鳥居を建設し皇大神宮の遙拝所とした「神社明細帳（全州神社）」 JACAR（アジア歴史資料センター） Ref.A03010213400 （国立公文書館）。 [[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）、在住日本人の間で全州神社を創立することで衆議が決定したため、道内で寄付を集め、同年[[11月24日]]に本殿以下の建物が竣工した。[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[2月21日]]、諸設備の完成を待って神社創立を出願し、神社寺院規則（大正4年朝鮮総督府令第82号）に基づいて、同年[[9月27日]]、天照大神を祭神とする全州神社の創立が許可された神社創立許可 朝鮮総督府官報第1248号（大正5年9月29日） 424頁。 [[1935年]]（昭和10年）[[11月28日]]、明治天皇と国魂大神を増祀し、[[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[8月11日]]に道供進社（道より供進することを得るべき神社）に指定され朝鮮総督府告示第440号 朝鮮総督府官報第2874号（昭和11年8月11日） 83頁、紀元2600年記念事業として移転改築を行うべく、[[1938年]]（昭和13年）7月には奉賛会を組織したが、時局のために工事は思うように進まず、完成には至らなかった。 [[1944年]]（昭和19年）[[5月1日]]、国幣小社に列格された朝鮮総督府告示第674号 朝鮮総督府官報第5169号（昭和19年5月2日） 5頁が、[[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[第二次世界大戦]][[敗戦]]に伴い、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[11月17日]]に廃止された内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁。 ==脚注== {{DEFAULTSORT:せんしゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない建築物]] {{shinto-stub}} [[zh:全州神社]]

{{神社| 名称=全州神社| 画像 =[[Image:Zenshu Shrine.JPG|250px|center]]| 所在地=朝鮮全羅北道全州府華山面| 祭神=天照大神

revision.comment: ボット: 言語間リンク 1 件を[[Wikipedia:ウィキデータ|ウィキデータ]]上の [[d:Q10891572]] に転記
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=全州神社| 画像 =[[Image:Zenshu Shrine.JPG|250px|center]]| 所在地=朝鮮全羅北道全州府華山面| 祭神=天照大神
明治天皇
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）| 例祭=10月14日}} '''全州神社'''（ぜんしゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[全羅北道 (日本統治時代)|全羅北道]][[全州市|全州府]]（現・[[大韓民国]][[全羅北道]]全州市）にあった[[神社]]である。[[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[明治天皇]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]。旧[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）、府内の多佳山山頂に鳥居を建設し皇大神宮の遙拝所とした「神社明細帳（全州神社）」 JACAR（アジア歴史資料センター） Ref.A03010213400 （国立公文書館）。 [[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）、在住日本人の間で全州神社を創立することで衆議が決定したため、道内で寄付を集め、同年[[11月24日]]に本殿以下の建物が竣工した。[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[2月21日]]、諸設備の完成を待って神社創立を出願し、神社寺院規則（大正4年朝鮮総督府令第82号）に基づいて、同年[[9月27日]]、天照大神を祭神とする全州神社の創立が許可された神社創立許可 朝鮮総督府官報第1248号（大正5年9月29日） 424頁。 [[1935年]]（昭和10年）[[11月28日]]、明治天皇と国魂大神を増祀し、[[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[8月11日]]に道供進社（道より神饌幣帛料を供進すべき神社）に指定され朝鮮総督府告示第440号 朝鮮総督府官報第2874号（昭和11年8月11日） 83頁、紀元2600年記念事業として移転改築を行うべく、[[1938年]]（昭和13年）7月には奉賛会を組織したが、時局のために工事は思うように進まず、完成には至らなかった。 [[1944年]]（昭和19年）[[5月1日]]、国幣小社に列格された朝鮮総督府告示第674号 朝鮮総督府官報第5169号（昭和19年5月2日） 5頁が、[[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[第二次世界大戦]][[敗戦]]に伴い、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[11月17日]]に廃止された内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁。 ==脚注== {{DEFAULTSORT:せんしゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない建築物]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=全州神社| 画像 =[[Image:Zenshu Shrine.JPG|250px|center]]| 所在地=朝鮮全羅北道全州府華山面| 祭神=天照大神
明治天皇
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）| 例祭=10月14日}} '''全州神社'''（ぜんしゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[全羅北道 (日本統治時代)|全羅北道]][[全州市|全州府]]（現・[[大韓民国]][[全羅北道]]全州市）にあった[[神社]]である。[[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[明治天皇]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]。旧[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）、府内の多佳山山頂に鳥居を建設し皇大神宮の遙拝所とした「神社明細帳（全州神社）」 JACAR（アジア歴史資料センター） Ref.A03010213400 （国立公文書館）。 [[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）、在住日本人の間で全州神社を創立することで衆議が決定したため、道内で寄付を集め、同年[[11月24日]]に本殿以下の建物が竣工した。[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[2月21日]]、諸設備の完成を待って神社創立を出願し、神社寺院規則（大正4年朝鮮総督府令第82号）に基づいて、同年[[9月27日]]、天照大神を祭神とする全州神社の創立が許可された神社創立許可 朝鮮総督府官報第1248号（大正5年9月29日） 424頁。 [[1935年]]（昭和10年）[[11月28日]]、明治天皇と国魂大神を増祀し、[[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[8月11日]]に道供進社（道より神饌幣帛料を供進すべき神社）に指定され朝鮮総督府告示第440号 朝鮮総督府官報第2874号（昭和11年8月11日） 83頁、紀元2600年記念事業として移転改築を行うべく、[[1938年]]（昭和13年）7月には奉賛会を組織したが、時局のために工事は思うように進まず、完成には至らなかった。 [[1944年]]（昭和19年）[[5月1日]]、国幣小社に列格された朝鮮総督府告示第674号 朝鮮総督府官報第5169号（昭和19年5月2日） 5頁が、[[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[第二次世界大戦]][[敗戦]]に伴い、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[11月17日]]に廃止された内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁。 ==脚注== {{DEFAULTSORT:せんしゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない韓国の建築物]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{神社|名称=全州神社|画像=[[Image:Zenshu Shrine.JPG|250px|center]]|所在地=朝鮮全羅北道全州府華山面|祭神=天照大神
明治天皇
国魂大神|社格=国幣小社|創建=[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）|例祭=10月14日}}'''Zenshū Jinja''' was a [[Shinto Shrine]] located in [[Jeonju]]. Enshrined deities are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]], [[Emperor Meiji]], and Kunitama Omikami . The former company status was Kokuheishosha . It was founded in 1910 It was linked to [[State Shinto]] and Imperialism rather than local support for Shinto [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]]

It was founded in 1910 It was linked to [[State Shinto]] and Imperialism rather than local support for Shinto [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]]

revision.comment: ([[c:GR|GR]]) [[c:COM:FR|File renamed]]: [[File:Zenshu Shrine.JPG]] → [[File:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg]] [[c:COM:FR#FR2|Criterion 2]] (meaningless or ambiguous name)
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=全州神社 | 画像 =[[Image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | 所在地=朝鮮全羅北道全州府華山面 | 祭神=天照大神
明治天皇
国魂大神 | 社格=国幣小社 | 創建=[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年） | 例祭=10月14日 }} '''全州神社'''（ぜんしゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[全羅北道 (日本統治時代)|全羅北道]][[全州市|全州府]]華山面（現・[[大韓民国]][[全羅北道]][[完州郡]]）にあった[[神社]]である。[[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[明治天皇]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]。旧[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）、府内の多佳山山頂に鳥居を建設し皇大神宮の遙拝所とした「神社明細帳（全州神社）」 JACAR（アジア歴史資料センター） Ref.A03010213400 （国立公文書館）。 [[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）、在住日本人の間で全州神社を創立することで衆議が決定したため、道内で寄付を集め、同年[[11月24日]]に本殿以下の建物が竣工した。[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[2月21日]]、諸設備の完成を待って神社創立を出願し、神社寺院規則（大正4年朝鮮総督府令第82号）に基づいて、同年[[9月27日]]、天照大神を祭神とする全州神社の創立が許可された神社創立許可 朝鮮総督府官報第1248号（大正5年9月29日） 424頁。 [[1935年]]（昭和10年）[[11月28日]]、明治天皇と国魂大神を増祀し、[[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[8月11日]]に道供進社（道より神饌幣帛料を供進すべき神社）に指定され朝鮮総督府告示第440号 朝鮮総督府官報第2874号（昭和11年8月11日） 83頁、紀元2600年記念事業として移転改築を行うべく、[[1938年]]（昭和13年）7月には奉賛会を組織したが、時局のために工事は思うように進まず、完成には至らなかった。 [[1944年]]（昭和19年）[[5月1日]]、国幣小社に列格された朝鮮総督府告示第674号 朝鮮総督府官報第5169号（昭和19年5月2日） 5頁が、[[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[第二次世界大戦]][[敗戦]]に伴い、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[11月17日]]に廃止された内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁。 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せんしゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない韓国の建築物]] [[Category:神明神社|廃せんしゆうしんしや]] [[Category:完州郡]] [[Category:1910年設立]]

{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916}}'''Zenshū Jinja''' was a [[Shinto Shrine]] located in [[Jeonju]]. Enshrined deities are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]], [[Emperor Meiji]], and Kunitama Omikami . The former shrine status was [[Kokuheishosha]]. It was linked to [[State Shinto]] and Imperialism rather than local support for Shinto {{Shrine | Name=Zenshu Shrine | Image=[[Image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | Location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | Deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | Shrine Rank=National Shrine, Minor Rank | Founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | Annual Festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kokudama Omikami|ja|国魂}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Imperial Grand Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{Notes help}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Kk]][[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

Kokudama Omikami | Shrine Rank=National Shrine, Minor Rank | Founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | Annual Festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kokudama Omikami|ja|国魂}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Imperial Grand Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{Notes help}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Original page]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916}}'''Zenshū Jinja''' was a [[Shinto Shrine]] located in [[Jeonju]]. Enshrined deities are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]], [[Emperor Meiji]], and Kunitama Omikami . The former shrine status was [[Kokuheishosha]]. It was linked to [[State Shinto]] and Imperialism rather than local support for Shinto {{Shrine | Name=Zenshu Shrine | Image=[[Image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | Location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | Deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | Shrine Rank=National Shrine, Minor Rank | Founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | Annual Festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kokudama Omikami|ja|国魂}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Imperial Grand Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{Notes help}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Original page]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

It was linked to [[State Shinto]] and Imperialism rather than local support for Shinto '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kokudama Omikami|ja|国魂}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Imperial Grand Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:original pages]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image=[[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshū Jinja''' was a [[Shinto Shrine]] located in [[Jeonju]]. Enshrined deities are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]], [[Emperor Meiji]], and Kunitama Omikami . The former shrine status was [[Kokuheishosha]]. It was linked to [[State Shinto]] and Imperialism rather than local support for Shinto '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kokudama Omikami|ja|国魂}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Imperial Grand Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTZenshū Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image=[[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshū Jinja''' was a [[Shinto Shrine]] located in [[Jeonju]]. Enshrined deities are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]], [[Emperor Meiji]], and Kunitama Omikami . The former shrine status was [[Kokuheishosha]]. It was linked to [[State Shinto]] and Imperialism rather than local support for Shinto '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kokudama Omikami|ja|国魂}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Imperial Grand Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTZenshū Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshū Jinja''' was a [[Shinto Shrine]] located in [[Jeonju]]. Enshrined deities are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]], [[Emperor Meiji]], and Kunitama Omikami . The former shrine status was [[Kokuheishosha]]. It was linked to [[State Shinto]] and Imperialism rather than local support for Shinto '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kokudama Omikami|ja|国魂}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Imperial Grand Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

It was linked to [[State Shinto]] and Imperialism rather than local support for Shinto '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image=[[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshū Jinja''' was a [[Shinto Shrine]] located in [[Jeonju]]. Enshrined deities are [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]], [[Emperor Meiji]], and Kunitama Omikami . The former shrine status was [[Kokuheishosha]]. It was linked to [[State Shinto]] and Imperialism rather than local support for Shinto '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image=[[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Zenshū Shrine-qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Zenshū Shrine-qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]]

On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Zenshū Shrine-qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Zenshū Shrine-qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image=[[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image=[[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a worship site for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Zenshū However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "worship site" to "{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}}"
revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image=[[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:enshū Shrine]][[Category:11]]
