revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image=[[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:enshū Shrine]][[Category:11]]

On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:enshū Shrine]][[Category:11]]

Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600年記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:enshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image=[[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[zh:全州神社]][[ja:全州神社]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:enshū Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=draft}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=draft}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=draft}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:全州神社]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}

Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|12=draft}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市|12=draft}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮|12=draft}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami|12=draft}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=draft}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=draft}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=draft}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:全州神社]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Original templates]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image=[[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|12=draft}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市|12=draft}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮|12=draft}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami|12=draft}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=draft}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=draft}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=draft}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:全州神社]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}

Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|12=draft}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市|12=draft}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮|12=draft}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=draft}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=draft}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=draft}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:全州神社]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}

Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=draft}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=draft}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=draft}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:全州神社]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image=[[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮|12=draft|12=draft}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami|12=draft|12=draft}}. It held the former rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=draft|12=draft}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=draft|12=draft}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}}

On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=draft|12=draft}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|12=draft" to "|qq=draft"
revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image=[[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{citation needed|Zenshū Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Zenshū Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: 18 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:全州神社 up to rev 99155396
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=全州神社 | 画像 =[[Image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] | 所在地=朝鮮全羅北道全州府華山面 | 祭神=天照大神
明治天皇
国魂大神 | 社格=国幣小社 | 創建=[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年） | 例祭=10月14日 }} '''全州神社'''（ぜんしゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[全羅北道 (日本統治時代)|全羅北道]][[全州市|全州府]]華山面（現・[[大韓民国]][[全羅北道]][[完州郡]]）にあった[[神社]]である。[[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[明治天皇]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]。旧[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）、府内の多佳山山頂に鳥居を建設し皇大神宮の遙拝所とした「神社明細帳（全州神社）」 JACAR（アジア歴史資料センター） Ref.A03010213400 （国立公文書館）。 [[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）、在住日本人の間で全州神社を創立することで衆議が決定したため、道内で寄付を集め、同年[[11月24日]]に本殿以下の建物が竣工した。[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[2月21日]]、諸設備の完成を待って神社創立を出願し、神社寺院規則（大正4年朝鮮総督府令第82号）に基づいて、同年[[9月27日]]、天照大神を祭神とする全州神社の創立が許可された神社創立許可 朝鮮総督府官報第1248号（大正5年9月29日） 424頁。 [[1935年]]（昭和10年）[[11月28日]]、明治天皇と国魂大神を増祀し、[[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[8月11日]]に道供進社（道より神饌幣帛料を供進すべき神社）に指定され朝鮮総督府告示第440号 朝鮮総督府官報第2874号（昭和11年8月11日） 83頁、紀元2600年記念事業として移転改築を行うべく、[[1938年]]（昭和13年）7月には奉賛会を組織したが、時局のために工事は思うように進まず、完成には至らなかった。 [[1944年]]（昭和19年）[[5月1日]]、国幣小社に列格された朝鮮総督府告示第674号 朝鮮総督府官報第5169号（昭和19年5月2日） 5頁が、[[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[第二次世界大戦]][[敗戦]]に伴い、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[11月17日]]に廃止された内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁。 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せんしゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない韓国の建築物]] [[Category:神明神社|廃せんしゆうしんしや]] [[Category:完州郡]] [[Category:1910年設立]]

Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|lt=Kunitama Omikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Zenshū Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]]

Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|qq=}} {{ill|Jeonju|ja|全州市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeonju|qq=}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese occupation of Korea|qq=}}. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|qq=}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|qq=}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|qq=}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:National Shrine, Minor Rank]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Wanju County]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Zenshū Shrine]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]]

On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|qq=}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|qq=}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:完州郡
revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image= | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|qq=}} [[:en:Jeonju|Jeonju]], Hwasan-myeon, during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The enshrined deities were [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]], and [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]]. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|qq=}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|qq=}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|qq=}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[en:Zenshū Shrine]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:完州郡]]

On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:完州郡]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]]

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image= | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 全北特別自治道}} [[:en:Jeonju|Jeonju]], Hwasan-myeon, during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The enshrined deities were [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]], and [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]]. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}. ==History== In [[1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:完州郡]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]]

Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Defunct structures in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:完州郡]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]]

It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}. ==History== In [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[:en:1914|1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[February 21]], [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:完州郡]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]]

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image= | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 全北特別自治道}} [[:en:Jeonju|Jeonju]], Hwasan-myeon, during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The enshrined deities were [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]], and [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]]. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}. ==History== In [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[:en:1914|1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[November 24]] of the same year. On [[:en:February 21|February 21]], [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[November 28]], [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[:en:August 11|August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[World War II]], the shrine was abolished on [[November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:完州郡]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]]

On [[:en:August 11|August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:完州郡]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]]
