{{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image= | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 全北特別自治道}} [[:en:Jeonju|Jeonju]], Hwasan-myeon, during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The enshrined deities were [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]], and [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]]. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}. ==History== In [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[:en:1914|1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[:en:November 24|November 24]] of the same year. On [[:en:February 21|February 21]], [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[:en:September 27|September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[:en:November 28|November 28]], [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[:en:August 11|August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:完州郡]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]]

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891572]] Wikidata name: Zenshū Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image= | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 全北特別自治道|1=North Jeolla|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41157}} [[:en:Jeonju|Jeonju]], Hwasan-myeon, during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The enshrined deities were [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]], and [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]]. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. ==History== In [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[:en:1914|1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[:en:November 24|November 24]] of the same year. On [[:en:February 21|February 21]], [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[:en:September 27|September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[:en:November 28|November 28]], [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[:en:August 11|August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]]

Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 全北特別自治道|1=North Jeolla|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41157}} [[:en:Jeonju|Jeonju]], Hwasan-myeon, during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The enshrined deities were [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]], and [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]]. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. ==History== In [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[:en:1914|1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[:en:November 24|November 24]] of the same year. On [[:en:February 21|February 21]], [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[:en:September 27|September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[:en:November 28|November 28]], [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[:en:August 11|August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|qq=|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[sn:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[sn:enshū Shrine]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Qq 4]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}}

Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[:en:November 24|November 24]] of the same year. On [[:en:February 21|February 21]], [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[:en:September 27|September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[:en:November 28|November 28]], [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[:en:August 11|August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}}

On [[:en:August 11|August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891572]] Wikidata name: Zenshū Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image= | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 全北特別自治道|1=North Jeolla|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41157}} [[:en:Jeonju|Jeonju]], Hwasan-myeon, during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The enshrined deities were [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]], and [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]]. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. ==History== In [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[:en:1914|1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[:en:November 24|November 24]] of the same year. On [[:en:February 21|February 21]], [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[:en:September 27|September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[:en:November 28|November 28]], [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[:en:August 11|August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
[[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891572]] Wikidata name: Zenshū Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image= | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 全北特別自治道|1=North Jeolla|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41157}} [[:en:Jeonju|Jeonju]], Hwasan-myeon, during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The enshrined deities were [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]], and [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]]. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. ==History== In [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[:en:1914|1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[:en:November 24|November 24]] of the same year. On [[:en:February 21|February 21]], [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[:en:September 27|September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[:en:November 28|November 28]], [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[:en:August 11|August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}}

revision.text:
[[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] [[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] [[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891572]] Wikidata name: Zenshū Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image= | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 全北特別自治道|1=North Jeolla|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41157}} [[:en:Jeonju|Jeonju]], Hwasan-myeon, during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The enshrined deities were [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]], and [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]]. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. ==History== In [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[:en:1914|1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[:en:November 24|November 24]] of the same year. On [[:en:February 21|February 21]], [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[:en:September 27|September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[:en:November 28|November 28]], [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[:en:August 11|August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}}

On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] [[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] [[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891572]] Wikidata name: Zenshū Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image= | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 全北特別自治道|1=North Jeolla|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41157}} [[:en:Jeonju|Jeonju]], Hwasan-myeon, during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The enshrined deities were [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]], and [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]]. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. ==History== In [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[:en:1914|1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[:en:November 24|November 24]] of the same year. On [[:en:February 21|February 21]], [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[:en:September 27|September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[:en:November 28|November 28]], [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[:en:August 11|August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] [[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] [[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891572]] Wikidata name: Zenshū Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image= | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded=[[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 全北特別自治道|1=North Jeolla|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41157}} [[:en:Jeonju|Jeonju]], Hwasan-myeon, during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The enshrined deities were [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]], and [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]]. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. ==History== In [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In [[:en:1914|1914]] (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on [[:en:November 24|November 24]] of the same year. On [[:en:February 21|February 21]], [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on [[:en:September 27|September 27]] of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On [[:en:November 28|November 28]], [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On [[:en:August 11|August 11]], [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in [[:en:1938|1938]] (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On [[:en:May 1|May 1]], [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine was abolished on [[:en:November 17|November 17]], [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on {{ill|November 24|en|November 24|WD=Q3022|lt=November 24}} of the same year. On {{ill|February 21|en|February 21|WD=Q2350|lt=February 21}}, {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}} (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on {{ill|September 27|en|September 27|WD=Q2884|lt=September 27}} of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On {{ill|November 28|en|November 28|WD=Q3017|lt=November 28}}, {{ill|1935|en|1935|WD=Q18658|lt=1935}} (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On {{ill|February 21|en|February 21|WD=Q2350|lt=February 21}}, {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}} (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on {{ill|September 27|en|September 27|WD=Q2884|lt=September 27}} of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On {{ill|November 28|en|November 28|WD=Q3017|lt=November 28}}, {{ill|1935|en|1935|WD=Q18658|lt=1935}} (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On {{ill|August 11|en|August 11|WD=Q2778|lt=August 11}}, {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936}} (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in {{ill|1938|en|1938|WD=Q18645|lt=1938}} (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1}}, {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, the shrine was abolished on {{ill|November 17|en|November 17|WD=Q3028|lt=November 17}}, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] [[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] [[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg}} {{Expand Japanese}}{{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image= | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded={{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}} (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 全北特別自治道|1=North Jeolla|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41157}} {{ill|Jeonju|en|Jeonju|WD=Q42140|lt=Jeonju}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Korea under Japanese rule|en|Korea under Japanese rule|WD=Q503585|lt=Japanese occupation of Korea}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|en|Kunitama|WD=Q9692242|lt=Kunitama Omikami}}. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. ==History== In {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910}} (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In {{ill|1914|en|1914|WD=Q2083|lt=1914}} (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on {{ill|November 24|en|November 24|WD=Q3022|lt=November 24}} of the same year. On {{ill|February 21|en|February 21|WD=Q2350|lt=February 21}}, {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}} (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on {{ill|September 27|en|September 27|WD=Q2884|lt=September 27}} of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On {{ill|November 28|en|November 28|WD=Q3017|lt=November 28}}, {{ill|1935|en|1935|WD=Q18658|lt=1935}} (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On {{ill|August 11|en|August 11|WD=Q2778|lt=August 11}}, {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936}} (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in {{ill|1938|en|1938|WD=Q18645|lt=1938}} (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1}}, {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=National Shrine, Minor Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, the shrine was abolished on {{ill|November 17|en|November 17|WD=Q3028|lt=November 17}}, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=Q9685496|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. ==History== In {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910|1=1910}} (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In {{ill|1914|en|1914|WD=Q2083|lt=1914|1=1914}} (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on {{ill|November 24|en|November 24|WD=Q3022|lt=November 24|1=November 24}} of the same year. On {{ill|February 21|en|February 21|WD=Q2350|lt=February 21|1=February 21}}, {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on {{ill|September 27|en|September 27|WD=Q2884|lt=September 27|1=September 27}} of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On {{ill|November 28|en|November 28|WD=Q3017|lt=November 28|1=November 28}}, {{ill|1935|en|1935|WD=Q18658|lt=1935|1=1935}} (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On {{ill|August 11|en|August 11|WD=Q2778|lt=August 11|1=August 11}}, {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in {{ill|1938|en|1938|WD=Q18645|lt=1938|1=1938}} (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1|1=May 1}}, {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=Q9685496|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine was abolished on {{ill|November 17|en|November 17|WD=Q3028|lt=November 17|1=November 17}}, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1|1=May 1}}, {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=Q9685496|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine was abolished on {{ill|November 17|en|November 17|WD=Q3028|lt=November 17|1=November 17}}, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] [[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] [[ja:全州神社]][[simple:Jeonju Shrine]][[zh:全州神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg}} {{infobox shinto shrine|name=Zenshū Shrine|native_name=全州神社|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|image=Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|location=Hwasan-myeon, Jeonju-bu, Jeollabuk-do, North Korea|deity=Amaterasu Omikami, Emperor Meiji, Kunitama Omikami|festival=October 14|year_completed=1916 | name=Zenshu Shrine | image= | location=Jeollabuk-do, Jeonju, Hwasan-myeon, Korea | deities=Amaterasu Omikami
Emperor Meiji
Kokudama Omikami | type=National Shrine, Minor Rank | founded={{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5) | festival=October 14 }} '''Zenshu Shrine''' (全州神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Jeollabuk-do|ja|全羅北道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jeollabuk-do|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 全北特別自治道|1=North Jeolla Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41157}} {{ill|Jeonju|en|Jeonju|WD=Q42140|lt=Jeonju|1=Jeonju}}, Hwasan-myeon, during the {{ill|Korea under Japanese rule|en|Korea under Japanese rule|WD=Q503585|lt=Japanese occupation of Korea|1=Korea under Japanese rule}}. The enshrined deities were {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}}, and {{ill|Kunitama|en|Kunitama|WD=Q9692242|lt=Kunitama Omikami|1=Kunitama}}. It held the former Rank of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=Q9685496|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. ==History== In {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910|1=1910}} (Meiji 43), a torii gate was erected at the summit of Takayama within the prefecture as a {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} for the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu (Inner Shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Details Ledger (Zenshu Shrine)" JACAR (National Archives of Japan) Ref.A03010213400 In {{ill|1914|en|1914|WD=Q2083|lt=1914|1=1914}} (Taishō 3), Japanese residents in the area resolved to establish Zenshu Shrine. Contributions were gathered within the prefecture, and the shrine's main buildings were completed on {{ill|November 24|en|November 24|WD=Q3022|lt=November 24|1=November 24}} of the same year. On {{ill|February 21|en|February 21|WD=Q2350|lt=February 21|1=February 21}}, {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5), after all facilities were finalized, an application for the shrine's establishment was submitted. Based on the {{ill|Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine and Temple Regulations|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taishō 4, Governor-General of Korea Ordinance No. 82), the establishment of Zenshu Shrine, enshrining Amaterasu Omikami, was approved on {{ill|September 27|en|September 27|WD=Q2884|lt=September 27|1=September 27}} of the same year."Approval for Shrine Establishment," Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 1248 (Taishō 5, September 29), p. 424 On {{ill|November 28|en|November 28|WD=Q3017|lt=November 28|1=November 28}}, {{ill|1935|en|1935|WD=Q18658|lt=1935|1=1935}} (Shōwa 10), Emperor Meiji and Kokudama Omikami were additionally enshrined. On {{ill|August 11|en|August 11|WD=Q2778|lt=August 11|1=August 11}}, {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dōkyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine supported with offerings of sacred food and cloth from the province).Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 440, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 2874 (Shōwa 11, August 11), p. 83 Plans to relocate and reconstruct the shrine as part of the {{ill|2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja|紀元2600th year 記念事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2600th-anniversary commemorative project|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were initiated in {{ill|1938|en|1938|WD=Q18645|lt=1938|1=1938}} (Shōwa 13) with the formation of a support association. However, due to prevailing circumstances, construction progressed poorly and was never completed. On {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1|1=May 1}}, {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine was elevated to the status of {{ill|National Shrine, Minor Rank|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine, Minor Rank|1=Q9685496|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Governor-General of Korea Notice No. 674, Governor-General of Korea Gazette No. 5169 (Shōwa 19, May 2), p. 5 Following Japan’s defeat in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine was abolished on {{ill|November 17|en|November 17|WD=Q3028|lt=November 17|1=November 17}}, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notice No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (Shōwa 20, November 22), p. 1 ==Notes== {{shinto-stub}} {{Korea-hist-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|全州神社|version=99155396|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Zenshu shrine torii.jpg|250px|center]] [[simple:Zenshū Shrine]] [[ja:全州神社]] [[zh:全州神社]] {{wikidata link|Q10891572}} [[Category:1910 establishments]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kanju District]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
